product.one_sentence_description: Launchframe is an OSS launch-control workspace for developer-tool launches.
product.launch_scope: Run the reusable OSS launch process end to end with low-risk real rehearsal surfaces.
product.monetization_summary: No business model is implemented for the dress rehearsal. A real Launchframe product would require sourced comparable research and owner approval before any pricing, hosted, enterprise, BYOK, or commercial claims.
product.ai_data_usage: No product AI data usage is implemented; Gemini/Nano Banana were used only for launch asset generation and review in this workspace.

surfaces.quickstart_command: Open http://launchframe.site/ or serve this repository locally with python3 -m http.server 4173.

policy_docs.data_retention_summary: Dress rehearsal uses local files and a local static server. No hosted user accounts, payments, telemetry, or persisted user data.

verification.core_falsifiable_demo: Launchframe turns scattered OSS launch work into an auditable gated workspace with context, checklists, visual cycles, repo/policy templates, and readiness reviews.
verification.demo_command: Review launch-context/00-MASTER-CHECKLIST.md, launch-context/00-org-context.json, launch-context/00-brand-context.md, index.html, and launch-context/11-DRESS-REHEARSAL-REPORT.md.
verification.expected_failure: A launch cannot be marked production-ready without real repo, package, policy, domain, community, install, and owner-approved public claims.
verification.expected_pass: Dress rehearsal readiness passes with low-risk real rehearsal surfaces while public posting remains unexecuted.

known_limitations: GitHub repository and GitHub Pages were created for real.
npm package publish reached the real registry after signed-browser token creation and isolated userconfig publish.
The published package was verified with npx.
Custom domain launchframe.site was purchased via Name.com and pointed at GitHub Pages; HTTPS certificate provisioning is pending.
Discord, LinkedIn, and HN posting remain rehearsal targets only.
hard_constraints: Do not buy domains, publish npm packages, post to HN/Reddit/social, or create community accounts without explicit approval.
Record which external steps actually executed and which stopped at an auth/approval gate.
Do not claim npm publish success until registry publish succeeds.
Do not claim HTTPS readiness until the custom-domain certificate is issued and enforcement is enabled.
open_questions: Should Launchframe become a real OSS product after this rehearsal?
If real, should the rehearsal targets become actual surfaces or be renamed?
